National Association of Science Writers and Council for the Advancement of Science Writing records
National Association of Science Writers
Lewenstein, Bruce V.
Council for the Advancement of Science Writing
4448
33 cubic feet. 33 cubic feet.
1934-2018. Records of the National Association of Science Writers and the Council for the Advancement of Science Writing include correspondence, membership records, financial records, domestic and international affairs material, newsletters, administrative material, working papers for seminars and workshops, samples of science writing, papers and theses, published reports, and video and audiocassettes. Correspondents include Earl Ubell, William J. Cromie, Pierre Fraley, Philip M. Boffey, Jane Stafford, and Herbert L. Nichols. Organizations mentioned include the American Association for the Advancement of Science and the Ford Foundation. ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
The National Association of Science Writers (NASW) is a professional society, established in 1934, serving science journalists and public information specialists. The Council for the Advancement of Science Writing (CASW), which was created in 1960, is a non-profit organization that sponsors various activities intended to improve the quality and quantity of information about science that reaches the public.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Series I. General files
Series II. Series II. International Affairs
Series III. Series III. Dometic Affairs
Cite As:
National Association of Science Writers and Council for the Advancement of Science Writing records, #4448. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Scope and content
Records of the National Association of Science Writers and the Council for the Advancement of Science Writing include correspondence, membership records, financial records, domestic and international affairs material, newlsetters, administrative material, working papers for seminars and workshops, samples of science writing, papers and theses, published reports, materials on the Science-in-Society Journalism Awards, and CDs, videotapes, and audiocassettes. Correspondents include Earl Ubell, William J. Cromie, Pierre Fraley, Philip M. Boffey, Jane Stafford, and Herbert L. Nichols. Organizations mentioned include the American Association for the Advancement of Science and the Ford Foundation. Also includes an oral history interview with Jane Stafford by Bruce Lewenstein, 1987.
Videotapes. Audiotapes. Journalism -- United States. Communication in science -- United States. Science news -- United States. Nichols, Herbert L. (Herbert Lownds), 1908- Fraley, Pierre. Boffey, Philip M. Stafford, Jane. Cromie, William J., 1930-2014. Ubell, Earl. Ford Foundation American Association for the Advancement of Science National Association of Science Writers Council for the Advancement of Science Writing
